perturbation.original_text: 2. Except as set forth in this Section 2, all Confidential Information shall be in tangible form and shall be marked as Confidential or proprietary information of the disclosing party. If the Confidential Information is disclosed orally or visually, it shall be identified as such at the time of disclosure and confirmed in a writing to the recipient within thirty (30) days of such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Except as set forth in this Section 2, all Confidential Information shall be in tangible form and shall be marked as Confidential or proprietary information of the disclosing party. If the Confidential Information is disclosed orally or visually, it shall be identified as such at the time of disclosure and confirmed in a writing to the recipient within fifteen (15) days of such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the timeframe for confirming orally or visually disclosed confidential information in writing. The original text specifies thirty (30) days, while the changed text reduces it to fifteen (15) days. This contradicts the recipient's obligations under Section 3, where the receiving party has different expectations depending on which version of the deadline they were following.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Each of the parties agrees that it will not make use of, disseminate, or in any way disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to any person, firm or business, except to the extent necessary for negotiations, discussions, and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of the other party and any purpose the other party may hereafter authorize in writing. Each of the parties agrees that it shall disclose Confidential Information of the other party only to those of its employees, consultants, advisors and investors who need to know such information and who have previously agreed, either as a condition to employment or in order to obtain the Confidential Information, to be bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the timeframe for confirming orally or visually disclosed confidential information from thirty (30) days to fifteen (15) days. This directly contradicts the recipient's obligations as they might rely on the original thirty-day period, creating an inconsistency in fulfilling their responsibilities as per Section 3. This falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, where conflicting deadlines are introduced.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Each of the parties agrees that it shall treat all Confidential Information of the other party with the same degree of care as it accords to its own Confidential Information, and each of the parties represents that it exercises reasonable care to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Each of the parties agrees that it shall treat all Confidential Information of the other party with a commercially reasonable degree of care, and each of the parties represents that it may, but is not required to, exercise reasonable care to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by weakening the standard of care required for protecting confidential information. Originally, parties were required to treat the other's confidential information with the same high level of care as their own. The altered text changes 'the same degree of care as it accords to its own' to 'a commercially reasonable degree of care'. Additionally, it now states the protection of one's own confidential information is optional. This creates conflict with Section 3, where the obligations to protect and not disclose are assumed to be under a high standard of care. The change creates uncertainty and contradicts that assumption.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Each of the parties agrees that it will not make use of, disseminate, or in any way disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to any person, firm or business, except to the extent necessary for negotiations, discussions, and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of the other party and any purpose the other party may hereafter authorize in writing. Each of the parties agrees that it shall disclose Confidential Information of the other party only to those of its employees, consultants, advisors and investors who need to know such information and who have previously agreed, either as a condition to employment or in order to obtain the Confidential Information, to be bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5 weakens the standard of care for protecting confidential information, stating that protection of one's own information is now optional. This directly contradicts Section 3, which assumes a high standard of care and mandatory protection of confidential information. The change creates a clear inconsistency and potential unenforceability issue, thus a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 8. This Agreement shall govern all communications between the parties that are made during the period from the effective date of this Agreement to the date on which either party receives from the other written notice that subsequent communications shall not be so governed, provided, however, that each party's obligations under Sections 2 and 3 with respect to Confidential Information of the other party which it has previously received shall continue unless and until such Confidential Information falls within Section 4.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. This Agreement shall govern all communications between the parties that are made during the period from the effective date of this Agreement to the date on which either party receives from the other written notice that subsequent communications shall not be so governed, provided, however, that each party's obligations under Sections 2 and 3 with respect to Confidential Information of the other party which it has previously received shall continue for a period of one (1) year, even if such Confidential Information falls within Section 4.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction related to the duration of confidentiality obligations. The original text states obligations under Sections 2 and 3 continue until the information falls within Section 4 (exceptions to confidentiality). The changed text limits these obligations to one year, regardless of whether the information falls under Section 4, directly contradicting the original condition and thus directly contradict Section 4 which states under which conditions there are no longer obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. There shall be no liability for disclosure or use of Confidential Information which is (a) in the public domain through no fault of the receiving party (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality, (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on use or disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party, (d) independently developed by the receiving party without use of any Confidential Information and by persons who have not had access to any Confidential Information (e) generally made available to third parties without any restriction on disclosure, or (f) communicated in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as otherwise required by law, or as necessary to establish the rights of either party under this Agreement (provided that the party so disclosing has provided the other party with a reasonable opportunity to seek protective legal treatment for such Confidential Information).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 8 by limiting the confidentiality obligations to one year, even if the Confidential Information falls within Section 4. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 4, which outlines conditions under which there is 'no liability for disclosure or use of Confidential Information'. The original text in Section 8 allowed the obligations to persist as long as the information did not fall under Section 4, while the changed text imposes a one-year limit regardless, which is a clear inconsistency and impacts enforceability of Section 4.

perturbation.original_text: 1. "Confidential Information" is that confidential, proprietary, and trade secret information being disclosed by the disclosing party pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. "Confidential Information" is that confidential, proprietary, and trade secret information being disclosed by the disclosing party pursuant to this Agreement, except for information related to pricing or marketing strategies.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement defines 'Confidential Information' broadly. The modified text adds an exception, excluding information related to pricing or marketing strategies. This creates a contradiction because other sections, like Section 3, apply to all 'Confidential Information'. With this perturbation, if pricing/marketing info is disclosed, it's unclear if those protections apply, creating uncertainty and conflicting with the intent of protecting all disclosed information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Each of the parties agrees that it will not make use of, disseminate, or in any way disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to any person, firm or business, except to the extent necessary for negotiations, discussions, and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of the other party and any purpose the other party may hereafter authorize in writing. Each of the parties agrees that it shall disclose Confidential Information of the other party only to those of its employees, consultants, advisors and investors who need to know such information and who have previously agreed, either as a condition to employment or in order to obtain the Confidential Information, to be bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text broadly defines 'Confidential Information.' The changed text introduces an exception for pricing/marketing strategies. Section 3 states that parties must not disclose 'any Confidential Information'. Since the changed text now excludes pricing/marketing strategies from the definition of 'Confidential Information', Section 3 no longer applies to this type of information, directly contradicting the original intent of protecting all disclosed information. This creates a clear contradiction because Section 3, as written, is meant to apply to ALL confidential information but now excludes certain types.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Each of the parties agrees that it will not make use of, disseminate, or in any way disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to any person, firm or business, except to the extent necessary for negotiations, discussions, and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of the other party and any purpose the other party may hereafter authorize in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Each of the parties agrees that it will not make use of, disseminate, or in any way disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to any person, firm or business, except to the extent necessary for negotiations, discussions, and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of the other party and any purpose the other party may hereafter authorize in writing. However, either party may disclose Confidential Information to governmental bodies or regulatory agencies without prior consent.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original restrictions on disclosing Confidential Information outlined in Section 3. The original clause prohibits disclosure except for specific purposes (negotiations, etc.). The addition allowing disclosure to governmental bodies without consent directly conflicts with this restriction, introducing an in-text contradiction. This directly affects Section 5 which now has to account for possible leaks to govermental bodies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Each of the parties agrees that it shall treat all Confidential Information of the other party with the same degree of care as it accords to its own Confidential Information, and each of the parties represents that it exercises reasonable care to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the confidentiality agreement, allowing disclosure to governmental bodies without prior consent, which directly contradicts the original clause prohibiting disclosure except for specific purposes. This conflicts with Section 5, which requires parties to treat confidential information with the same degree of care as their own, as the change introduces the possibility of leaks to governmental bodies without control by either party, thereby creating a strong contradiction.
